article.headline: Undav salvages a point for Stuttgart in 3-3 thriller with Heidenheim
article.description: Deniz Undav's thrilling equaliser eight minutes into added time salvaged a point for VfB Stuttgart, who settled for a 3-3 draw with Heidenheim in the Bundesliga on Sunday.
article.linkText: Undav salvages a point for Stuttgart in 3-3 thriller with Heidenheim

images.name: Deniz Undav scores goal for VfB Stuttgart
images.caption: Deniz Undav scores goal for VfB Stuttgart

video.headline: Deniz Undav scores goal for VfB Stuttgart
video.caption: Deniz Undav scores goal for VfB Stuttgart
video.title: Deniz Undav scores goal for VfB Stuttgart
video.description: Deniz Undav scores goal for VfB Stuttgart

tracking.trackingName: SOC_One-Play (Deniz Undav scores goal for VfB Stuttgart) 2024/03/31 ESHEET

article.story: STUTTGART, March 31 (Reuters) - Deniz Undav's thrilling equaliser eight minutes into added time salvaged a point for VfB Stuttgart, who settled for a 3-3 draw with Heidenheim in the Bundesliga on Sunday. Heidenheim fought back from two goals down to take the lead when Tim Kleindienst scored twice within a minute, but were not able to hold on to clinch their first win in six matches. The hosts took the lead in the 41st minute through Serhou Guirassy who scored from Angelo Stiller's cross from the left to take his goal tally to 23 in the league, second only to Bayern Munich's Harry Kane. Stiller doubled the lead in the 53rd minute, chasing Undav's through ball into the box and finishing with his left foot to earn his first goal for Stuttgart. Heidenheim got a goal back nine minutes later when Stuttgart keeper Alexander Nubel spilled Kleindienst's header off a corner into his own net. But Kleindienst got the equaliser for Heidenheim in the 84th minute, burying Jan-Niklas Beste's pass into the net with his left foot, scoring his first league goal since February. The forward struck again a minute later as Eren Dinkci set him up for a header from close range, sending thousands of visiting Heidenheim fans delirious with joy as the visitors took a 3-2 lead. But the hosts turned it around deep into added time when Maximilian Mittelstadt found Undav in the centre of the box for a right-footed finish. Heidenheim remain 11th with 30 points ahead of facing second-placed Bayern at home next weekend. Third placed Stuttgart, three points behind Bayern with 57 points, are four points ahead of fourth-placed Borussia Dortmund whom they visit on Saturday. (Reporting by Chiranjit Ojha in Bengaluru, editing by Pritha Sarkar)

keyEvents.text: Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 1, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 0. Serhou Guirassy (VfB Stuttgart) right footed shot from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Angelo Stiller.
Second Half begins VfB Stuttgart 1, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 0.
Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 2, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 0. Angelo Stiller (VfB Stuttgart) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Deniz Undav with a through ball.
Own Goal by Alexander Nübel, VfB Stuttgart.  VfB Stuttgart 2, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 1.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Atakan Karazor replaces Mahmoud Dahoud.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Jamie Leweling replaces Enzo Millot.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Kevin Sessa replaces Norman Theuerkauf.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Nikola Dovedan replaces Marvin Pieringer.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Silas Katompa Mvumpa replaces Chris Führich.

Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 2, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 2. Tim Kleindienst (1. FC Heidenheim 1846) left footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom right corner. Assisted by Jan-Niklas Beste with a cross.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Marnon Busch replaces Omar Traoré.

Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 2, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 3. Tim Kleindienst (1. FC Heidenheim 1846) header from very close range to the centre of the goal. Assisted by Eren Dinkçi with a cross   following a fast break.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Pascal Stenzel replaces Hiroki Ito.
Substitution, VfB Stuttgart. Jeong Woo-Yeong replaces Josha Vagnoman.
Substitution, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846. Lennard Maloney replaces Jan-Niklas Beste.
Waldemar Anton (VfB Stuttgart) is shown the yellow card.
Nikola Dovedan (1. FC Heidenheim 1846) is shown the red card.
Goal!  VfB Stuttgart 3, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 3. Deniz Undav (VfB Stuttgart) right footed shot from the centre of the box to the bottom left corner. Assisted by Maximilian Mittelstädt.
Second Half ends, VfB Stuttgart 3, 1. FC Heidenheim 1846 3.
